# onadiet

> Put your files on a diet — a local, no-upload CLI and embeddable engine that shrinks PDFs, images, and folders under a size limit, safe by default, with an honest before/after receipt.

onadiet runs entirely on your machine (zero network calls, no telemetry), never overwrites the original, never writes a bigger file, and measures every saving instead of estimating. It orchestrates best-in-class local encoders (sharp/libvips, qpdf, svgo) and verifies the output — keeping the original if it can't beat it honestly. Apache-2.0.

## Docs

- [Usage & API reference](https://onadiet.pages.dev/docs.html): commands, flags, the five diet plans, exit codes, and the four ways to run it (CLI, importable library, CI gate, agent Skill).
- [Why onadiet](https://onadiet.pages.dev/why.html): how it compares to TinyPNG, Squoosh, ImageOptim, and Ghostscript — on-device and free vs. upload-based or paid.
- [Benchmarks](https://onadiet.pages.dev/#numbers): measured before/after on real files (every figure reproducible with `pnpm run test:perf`).

## Install

- CLI (npm): `npm i -g onadiet` (binary `diet`), or run once with `npx onadiet report.pdf --to 5mb`.
- Library: `@onadiet/core` (pure engine) plus adapters `@onadiet/pdf`, `@onadiet/image`, `@onadiet/svg`.

## Source

- Repository: https://github.com/on-a-diet/onadiet (Apache-2.0)
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/onadiet
